methodology: Cross-reference network analysis (strongest connections in dataset: 17-18 votes) + patristic commentary reading (11 commentators across Catena Bible and CCEL) + topic verification (6 related topics) + linguistic pattern analysis (6 vocabulary patterns for spiritual blindness in Portuguese)

evidence: commentaries (Ambrosiaster, Dídimo the Blind, Chrysostom, Irenaeus, Tertullian, Jerome, Lactantius — 11 entries)

relevance_change.reason: Most cited OT verse on blindness in the NT (6 citations confirmed by Haydock). 17 votes to Mt 13:14. Foundational OT text for the entire 'spiritual blindness' theme.

new_refs_added.justification: Hub central da rede: 17 votes de 2CO.4.4, 13 votes de ISA.6.9 e ISA.6.10. Cita Isaías 6:10 diretamente no contexto joanino. Cornelius a Lapide confirma explicitamente. Texto: 'Cegou-lhes os olhos e endureceu-lhes o coração.' Verso-ponte que conecta a profecia de Isaías ao ministério de Jesus.

8 votes de 2CO.4.4. 5 votes de MAT.13.14. Johnson conecta explicitamente: 'véu de cegueira cairá.' Metáfora do véu = mecanismo que explica COMO a cegueira opera. Complementa 2Co 4:4 (quem cega) com o como (véu sobre a mente).

9 votes de ISA.6.9. Ambrosiaster distingue dois tipos de cegueira (permanente vs temporária). Johnson cita Is 6:9 e Mt 13:14-15 no comentário. Tópico UNBELIEF. Texto: 'Deus lhes deu espírito de entorpecimento, olhos para não ver.'

candidates_noted.justification: Crossref 3v→2CO.4.4. Tópico BLINDNESS_SPIRITUAL lista Ef 4:17-19. 'Obscurecidos de entendimento, alheios à vida de Deus pela dureza do coração.'
3 votes de JHN.9.39. Tópico BLINDNESS_SPIRITUAL. Vocabulário explícito: 'trevas cegaram os olhos.' Conecta cegueira → ódio ao irmão.
8 votes de ISA.6.9. Última citação de Is 6:9-10 no NT (Paulo em Roma). Johnson: 'citada 6 vezes no NT, sempre aplicada à incredulidade judaica.'

isaiah_6_citation_chain.description: Isaiah 6:9-10 is cited 6 times in the NT, confirmed by Haydock (AD 1849)

citations.context: Jesus explains why he uses parables
John's theological summary of Jewish unbelief
Paul's final address to Roman Jews

isaiah_6_citation_chain.significance: The most quoted OT passage on spiritual perception in the NT. Any hybrid search for 'cegueira espiritual' that doesn't retrieve at least 3-4 of these citations is failing at cross-testament linking.

vocabulary_patterns.description: 6 distinct Portuguese vocabulary patterns for spiritual blindness
vocabulary_patterns.significance_for_tcc: Lexical search for 'cegueira' finds only pattern 1 (4 verses). Semantic search must understand that patterns 2-6 express the same concept with different vocabulary. This query is the strongest test case for semantic superiority over lexical.

commentator_contributions.name: Chrysostom
commentator_contributions.unique: Doctrine of 'two blindnesses' (sensible and spiritual). Emphasizes voluntary self-blinding.

commentator_contributions.name: Cyril of Alexandria
commentator_contributions.unique: Connects John 9:39 → Isaiah explicitly. Distinguishes involuntary ignorance from deliberate rejection.

commentator_contributions.name: Augustine
commentator_contributions.unique: Blindness → election → grace connection. 'They did not believe because they did not will it.'

commentator_contributions.name: Jerome
commentator_contributions.unique: 'They closed their eyes' = act of will, not natural deficiency.

commentator_contributions.name: Ambrosiaster
commentator_contributions.unique: Two types: permanent vs temporary blindness. God 'dims' the hostile.

commentator_contributions.name: Didymus the Blind
commentator_contributions.unique: Physically blind scholar commenting on spiritual blindness. 'Every unbeliever is of this world.'

commentator_contributions.name: Thomas Watson
commentator_contributions.unique: 'Satan leaves men their left eye [worldly knowledge] but plucks out the right eye [spiritual knowledge].'
